LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT P.G.H.C.C., INC. DBA PACIFIC GROVE CONVALESCENT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO RE-FILING THE MOTION. PGHCC DID NOT SATISFY ITS BURDEN OF PRODUCTION. PLAINTIFFS' OBJECTIONS TO THE DECLARATION OF MS. MULVEY ARE SUSTAINED. THE DECLARATION DOES NOT SHOW THAT IT WAS MADE ON PERSONAL KNOWLEDGE AND DOES NOT SUFFICIENTLY IDENTIFY THE RECORDS. PLAINTIFFS' OBJECTIONS TO THE DECLARATION OF MS. INGRAM ARE SUSTAINED. THE MEDICAL RECORDS THAT MS. INGRAM RELIES ON ARE NOT PROPERLY BEFORE THE COURT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR # 5976

Off Calendar Jun-26-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Sandra K. McIntyre, Esq., of Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard & Smith, Tel. 415.438.5917, appearing for defendant and moving party.
Steven A. Fabbro, Esq., of the Law Office of Steven A. Fabbro, Tel. 415.391.6850, appearing for plaintiff and opposing party.
Jennifer A. Wagster, Esq., of Hinshaw, Draa, Marsh, Still & Hinshaw, Tel. 408.861.6500, appearing for defendant Dr. Tocchet via court call.

The matter was argued. The court adopted its tentative ruling as modified: DEFENDANT P.G.H.C.C., INC. DBA PACIFIC GROVE CONVALESCENT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is Denied without prejudice to re-filing the motion. PGHCC did not satisfy its burden of production. Plaintiffs' objections to the declaration of Ms. Mulvey are sustained. The declaration does not show that it was made on personal knowledge and does not sufficiently identify the records. Plaintiffs' objections to the declaration of Ms. Ingram are sustained. The medical records that Ms. Ingram relies on are not properly before the court. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR # 5976
